title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Irvington, NY | $900–$2,500 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Irvington, Westchester. Safe, humane bat extraction and sealing. Licensed experts. Call for fast service: $900–$2,500.
intro: Irvington's historic homes and tree-lined streets provide ideal roosting spots for bats in attics, chimneys, and eaves. Best Pest Control NYC offers comprehensive bat removal and exclusion services throughout Irvington, ensuring your property is safely and humanely cleared of bats. Our licensed technicians use proven methods to remove bats and seal entry points, protecting your Westchester home year-round.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has extensive experience serving Irvington and the greater Westchester area with humane, effective bat removal solutions. Our team is fully licensed, insured, and trained in New York wildlife regulations, ensuring safe removal and long-term exclusion that protects both your family and local bat populations.
localContext: Irvington's beautiful older homes near the Hudson River and tree-canopied neighborhoods like the Irvington Woods area are particularly attractive to bat colonies seeking shelter. The village's historic architecture, including properties near the Old Croton Aqueduct Trail and Main Street, often feature the gaps, vents, and chimneys that bats exploit for nesting.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Irvington?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Irvington typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your home's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before any work begins.

faqs.question: When is bat season in Irvington, NY?
faqs.answer: Bats are most active in Irvington from April through October. However, maternity season from May through August requires special care, as flightless baby bats cannot be excluded. Our team follows all New York State regulations to ensure humane removal timing.

faqs.question: Are bats common in Irvington homes?
faqs.answer: Yes, Irvington's proximity to the Hudson River, wooded areas, and abundance of historic homes with attics and chimneys make it a hotspot for bat activity. Little brown bats and big brown bats are the most common species found roosting in local residences.

faqs.question: What does bat exclusion involve?
faqs.answer: Bat exclusion involves a thorough inspection to locate all entry points, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, and permanent sealing of all gaps, vents, and openings. We also provide attic cleanup and sanitation services to remove guano and contamination if needed.
